filter_pushdown.mechanism: zone_map_block_skip — skip all blocks where block_max < 8766 OR block_min >= 9131; only ~90 of 600 blocks need I/O

filter_pushdown.note: applied per-row within qualifying blocks after shipdate check; 3 of 11 distinct values qualify
applied last; l_extendedprice loaded ONLY for rows passing all three predicates (late materialization)

data_structures.aggregation: thread_local_double_accumulator — one double per thread; final reduction is a sequential sum over 32 values; no hash table needed (scalar SUM, no GROUP BY)

parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_accumulator_then_sequential_merge — zero contention during scan; main thread sums 32 doubles at the end
parallelism.notes: Distribute the ~90 qualifying block indices across 32 threads before launch. Each thread iterates its assigned blocks, applies fused predicates, accumulates into a local double. No atomics needed.

index_usage.layout: uint32_t num_blocks, then per-block: int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t block_size (12 bytes each)
index_usage.load_method: mmap entire 7204-byte file; iterate all 600 entries; collect qualifying block indices into a flat vector before launching threads

column_load_order.note: within a qualifying block the zone map guarantees overlap but individual rows near block boundaries may still miss — check per row
load only for rows passing shipdate
load only for rows passing shipdate AND discount

column_load_order.predicate: none — multiply with already-loaded l_discount and add to accumulator
column_load_order.note: late materialization — loaded only for the ~1.15M qualifying rows; avoids reading 480MB of extendedprice data for rows that will be rejected

inner_loop_strategy.description: Fused scan loop over each qualifying block. For each row i in [row_offset, row_offset+block_size): check shipdate, then discount, then quantity; on triple-pass multiply extendedprice*discount and add to local_sum.
inner_loop_strategy.branch_prediction: early-exit per predicate; discount and quantity checks short-circuit ~81% of shipdate-passing rows; SIMD gather can be applied to discount/quantity arrays for vectorized predicate evaluation
inner_loop_strategy.simd_opportunity: All filter columns are double (64-bit) or int32. AVX2 processes 4 doubles or 8 int32s per register. Discount and quantity predicates can be vectorized as 4-wide double comparisons.

physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential_within_qualifying_blocks — mmap all four column files; OS prefetcher handles sequential access within each 100K-row block; non-qualifying blocks are never touched (no page faults)

estimated_cost.data_volume_note: ~90 blocks × 100K rows × (4B shipdate + 8B discount + 8B quantity) = ~180MB for filter columns; extendedprice adds ~9.2MB for the 1.15M qualifying rows (late mat.)
estimated_cost.bottleneck: memory_bandwidth_and_predicate_evaluation — HDD latency avoided by mmap+OS page cache on repeated runs; 32-thread parallelism amortizes scan cost
estimated_cost.notes: Zone map cuts I/O by 85%. Late materialization avoids ~480MB of extendedprice reads. Thread-local accumulators eliminate all synchronization during the scan. Target: match or beat DuckDB (20ms), approach Umbra (14ms).
